actual: The term "sink syntax" refers to a pattern in ARO where data flows into a sink — a sink is a sink because it accepts data but does not emit it further. Sink syntax is a pattern that allows you to sink data into a sink without further processing. Sink syntax is used to sink data into a sink without further processing. expected: Sink syntax (ARO-0043) lets you put an expression in the *result* position instead of a binding name. The expression is evaluated and its value flows on. Useful when you want to compute and immediately consume without naming the intermediate.
